The Maharashtra Electricity Tariff Act, 2016
Order of the Maharashtra Electricity Regulatory Commission No. 75 dated 27th March, 2026
The Proposal:
Under the Maharashtra Electricity Tariff Act, 2016, all types of electricity consumers in the state are required to pay electricity bills.
Electricity duty is charged on the electricity consumed. In the Act
By definition, "consumption charges" include fixed charges, currency charges, and fuel adjustments.
Includes assessment and reliability assessment.
In recent times, Rooftop Solar (RTS) has been supported by Central and State Government subsidy schemes.
The number of projects is increasing exponentially. Also, electricity in the form of Behind the Meter (BTM)
Manufacturing (captive, storage-based systems, etc.) is also on the rise. This results in the consumption of conventional electricity.
The system is changing, and it is necessary to reform the way electricity tariffs are applied.

A committee is being constituted to study the power tariff structure as follows.
The Chairman of the said Committee shall, as required, appoint the Inspector of Electricity Charges and other officers / staff.
They may be invited to the meeting.
The functions of the said Committee shall be as follows: -
The committee is mandated to levy electricity duty under the Maharashtra Electricity Tariff Act, 2016.

To make recommendations on the application of ED to on-going electricity and grid support charges.
To explore the possibility of developing a percentage-based ED structure instead of fixed rates.
